sections.opening: Sit down, dear one, the candle is lit and the heart is open. After we learned about the first chapters of Psalms and the virtue of age, it's time to enter the wonderful valley of selected Psalms that accompany us in daily life. Chapters 18-34 are a true treasure of prayers for every situation. Psalm 18 brings salvation from enemies, 19 strengthens faith, 20 brings salvation in times of trouble, 21 grants blessing and success, 22 gives strength in great distress, 23 brings livelihood and abundance, 24 brings success in business, and so on. Each chapter is like a small key to a great door in the upper worlds. The Baal Shem Tov said that when a person recites Psalms, they don't just pray — they change reality. Today we'll discover how to use these keys wisely.
sections.theoryA:
How do you choose which Psalm to read?

The right choice of Psalm chapter depends on what you feel and what you need right now. Each Psalm chapter is like a precise spiritual remedy for a specific spiritual condition. Chapter 18 'I will love You, O Lord, my strength' is for times when you feel surrounded by enemies or in a difficult situation requiring salvation. Its virtue is salvation from harsh decrees and enemies. The message is that the Lord is our rock and fortress, the place of true security.

Chapter 19 'The heavens declare the glory of God' is perfect when you want to strengthen faith. Its virtue is strengthening faith and fear of Heaven, and the message is that creation testifies to the Creator and that Torah restores the soul. Chapter 20 'May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble' is exactly what the name says — for a day of trouble. Its virtue is for livelihood and salvation on a day of judgment.

Chapter 21 brings blessing and success, 22 gives strength in great distress like illness or mourning, 23 is the most famous chapter for livelihood and abundance ('The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want'), 24 brings success in business and commerce. There's precise spiritual logic here — each spiritual situation requires an appropriate 'frequency,' and each Psalm chapter operates on a different spiritual frequency.

practice1.instruction: Take your Book of Psalms now. Choose one situation that's troubling you these days — fear, livelihood, health, or relationships. Find the appropriate chapter from the list above. Read the chapter once aloud, slowly, and pay attention to which verse touches you most.

sections.theoryB:
What's the difference between different Psalm chapters?

Each Psalm chapter was written from a different state of mind and addresses a different higher power. Chapters 25-34 represent a transition from repentance to joy. Chapter 25 'To You, O Lord, I lift up my soul' is complete repentance and forgiveness of sins. Here King David turns to the Lord from deep recognition that he needs guidance. Chapter 26 'Judge me, O Lord, for I have walked in my integrity' is for vindication in judgment — suitable for one facing court or needing to prove innocence.

Chapter 27 'The Lord is my light and salvation, whom shall I fear' is one of the strongest chapters against fear. This is the chapter of the month of Elul and the High Holy Days, a chapter that gives confidence and courage. Chapter 28 brings peace with enemies and cancels hatred, 29 brings domestic peace and family harmony.

Chapter 30 is special thanksgiving — 'A Psalm, a song of dedication of the House by David.' This is the chapter recited after healing or when dedicating a new home. Chapters 31-32 move from confidence during crisis to joy of confession and atonement. Chapter 33 is a new song — blessing for children and everything new that's born. Chapter 34 'Taste and see that the Lord is good' is the summary — protection from all evil, grace and success.

practice2.instruction: Choose three chapters from the list that seem most relevant to your life right now. Write next to each chapter how it connects to something specific in your life. For example: 'Chapter 23 - I'm worried about livelihood this year' or 'Chapter 27 - I have fears about the future.' This will be your personal list.

sections.deepDive:
The Hidden Secret of the Selected Psalms

What's not written in books is that each of these selected Psalm chapters operates not only on your soul, but also on the environment. The Baal Shem Tov discovered that when a person recites Psalms with intention, they change the energy field around them. Chapter 18, for example, doesn't just bring you salvation from enemies — it causes enemies to forget about you.

There's a hidden order in these chapters. 18-21 are the 'cycle of strength' — salvation, faith, deliverance and blessing. 22-25 are the 'cycle of purification' — great distress, abundance, holiness and repentance. 26-31 are the 'cycle of confidence' — vindication in judgment, courage, peace, joy, thanksgiving and confidence in crisis. 32-34 are the 'cycle of joy' — confession, new song and protection from all evil.

The holy ones discovered that if you read these four cycles in sequence, on Shabbat or Rosh Chodesh, it brings complete blessing to all of life. It's like undergoing complete energy healing with the help of the holy words of King David.

questions.question: Which Psalm chapter is suitable for salvation from enemies?
questions.explanation: Chapter 18 'I will love You, O Lord, my strength' is the chapter for salvation from enemies and harsh decrees, where David says 'The Lord is my rock and my fortress.'

questions.question: What is the virtue of Chapter 23 'The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want'?
questions.explanation: Chapter 23 is the most famous chapter for livelihood and abundance, because it expresses complete trust that one who trusts in the Lord will lack nothing.

questions.question: Which chapter is suitable for complete repentance and forgiveness of sins?
questions.explanation: Chapter 25 'To You, O Lord, I lift up my soul' is for complete repentance and forgiveness of sins, where David asks the Lord to guide him in the right path.

questions.question: What's special about Chapter 27 in relation to fear?
questions.explanation: Chapter 27 'The Lord is my light and salvation, whom shall I fear' is one of the strongest chapters against fear, giving confidence and courage.

questions.question: According to the Baal Shem Tov, what happens when a person recites Psalms with intention?
questions.explanation: The Baal Shem Tov taught that when a person recites Psalms with intention, they don't just pray but actually change reality and spiritual influences.

closing.summary: Today we learned about the selected Psalms chapters 18-34, how each chapter addresses a different life situation and how to choose the right chapter. We saw there are four cycles: strength, purification, confidence and joy. We understood that Psalms not only affect the soul but change the energy field.
closing.wisdom: King David knew that for every situation there is a key, and for every key there is a Psalm.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we'll discover the hidden secret of Psalm 119 — the longest chapter in Psalms and how to read it according to the letters of the Name.

faq.question: How many times should each Psalm chapter be read?
faq.answer: There's no fixed rule. Some read once with great intention, some read three or seven times. The main thing is intention and an open heart.

faq.question: Can you read several chapters together?
faq.answer: Absolutely. In fact, it's recommended to read chapters that connect to the same topic. For example, chapters 20-21 together for livelihood, or 27-28 together for confidence and peace.

faq.question: What's better — reading many chapters quickly or one chapter slowly?
faq.answer: It's better to read one chapter slowly, with intention and understanding, than many chapters quickly without focus. Psalms work through intention and the heart.

faq.question: Is there a special time of day to read Psalms?
faq.answer: Psalms are always good, but special times are: after morning prayers, before Shabbat, and in times of trouble. Chapter 27 is also read during the month of Elul.

seo.title: Selected Psalms — Chapters 18-34 for Life Situations | Maktuba Academy
seo.description: Learn how to choose the right Psalm chapter for every situation: salvation, livelihood, confidence and peace. Practical guide to Psalms 18-34 with virtues and intentions for each chapter.
